understanding.current_goal: Trace every claim in contracts/DETERMINISM.md to the code that implements it and assess satisfaction.
understanding.latest_decision: Use topological sort for CSS (accepted) — CSS blocks are ordered by dependency depth via compiler sort.
understanding.active_task: Verify CSS determinism contract (active) — ID: rec_task_01KY3Y896HCXNDZFBHWXQFHV2N | revision: 1
understanding.next_action: Read the compiler-side CSS extraction and module-ID canonicalization sources to verify upstream ordering and normalization guarantees.

findings.claim: CSS styles are ordered by dependency depth (reverse topological sort): if A imports B and B imports C, output order is C, B, A.
findings.notes: bundle.rs's fallback graph traversal performs a post-order DFS that yields [C, B, A]. However, the main path uses compiled.style_blocks directly and process_css merely concatenates in input order — it does not verify or enforce topological order. The compiler-side sort has not been inspected, and css_determinism.rs comments that process_css 'expects input to be ALREADY topologically sorted'.

findings.claim: Byte-stability: line endings normalized to \n before hashing and concatenation; \r\n forbidden in final output; UTF-8 enforced.
findings.notes:
compile_zen_source normalizes source 
 to 
, and extract_style_blocks_from_source_file normalizes style block content. process_css, however, does not re-check that final CSS contains no 
, nor does it enforce UTF-8. The cross-OS test explicitly assumes a compiler/graph 'strict_normalize_css' step that is not present in the provided files.

findings.claim: HTML must contain exactly one <!-- ZENITH_STYLES_ANCHOR -->; bundler hard-fails on missing, multiple, or malformed anchors.
findings.notes: process_css in the main bundle.rs path returns Err when anchor count != 1, satisfying the hard-fail. bundler_html_emit.rs inject_stylesheet_link_once contains an alternate path that falls back to replacing a single existing stylesheet link when the anchor is missing, which directly contradicts the hard-fail requirement if that helper is used by any code path.

findings.claim: CSS bundle is constructed via simple concatenation of topologically sorted blocks.
findings.notes: process_css concatenates block contents in input order with a stable '\n\n' separator. No specificity, filename, or optimization reordering occurs in the extraction phase.
process_css never checks for uniqueness of (module_id, order). Duplicate pairs would simply produce duplicate concatenated content and duplicate hashes without raising an error.

findings.claim: Duplicate content from different modules is preserved.
findings.notes: Each block is pushed into clean_content independently, so identical CSS from distinct modules is included twice.

findings.claim: No reordering based on specificity or file names, and no implicit optimization/minification in CSS extraction.
findings.notes: No specificity/file-name logic exists. Minification in execute_bundle is configured only for the Rolldown JS bundle; CSS extraction is not minified.

findings.claim: Canonical module IDs use forward slashes; relative imports resolved relative to canonical importer path; graph hashes identical across OSes.
findings.location: packages/bundler/src/utils.rs clean_path; packages/bundler/src/plugin/zenith_loader.rs resolve_id; packages/bundler/src/bundle.rs resolve_relative_specifier / normalize_path
findings.notes: clean_path normalizes backslashes and textual . / .. segments, but resolve_id and bundle.rs's graph resolution use std::fs::canonicalize and PathBuf::to_string_lossy, which can produce OS-native separators on Windows. There is no evidence that ExtractedStyleBlock::module_id values are uniformly forward-slash normalized.

findings.claim: Expression extraction order is deterministic (left-to-right, depth-first) and __zenith_expr matches source appearance.
findings.notes: Tests confirm expression order for simple cases, and generate_virtual_entry emits the expression array in order. The compiler's actual extraction traversal is not provided in the evidence, so full verification is not possible.

findings.claim: Unified pipeline: zenith dev uses the same zenith-bundler binary and pipeline as zenith build; no fast-path bypass; CSS in dev equals prod except optional minification.
findings.notes: All modes flow through execute_bundle. Dev only adds an HMR footer via transform (JS only) and may enable JS minification in prod. process_css is mode-agnostic, so dev and prod CSS bytes are identical.

findings.claim: zenith:css:<page> and zenith:entry:<page> virtual modules are sealed; user code cannot mock or override them.
findings.notes: reject_external_zenith_import rejects user imports starting with \0 or containing escaped zenith namespace strings. resolve_id only resolves .zen files and internal \0zenith:* IDs, keeping virtual modules hermetically sealed.

patch.next: Inspect the compiler sources that produce ExtractedStyleBlock and the module graph ordering (e.g., packages/compiler/src/script.rs / graph.rs) to confirm topological ordering and CRLF/UTF-8 normalization happen before process_css receives the blocks.

update.reason: The bundler side preserves order and rejects bad anchors, but the actual topological sort and byte-normalization of CSS blocks are claimed to originate in the compiler/graph layer, which has not yet been examined.

stopping_point: Completed mapping of all DETERMINISM.md claims to the provided bundler source files. Stopped before reading compiler-side style extraction / graph canonicalization code, and before tracing every caller of inject_stylesheet_link_once.
completed: Read contracts/DETERMINISM.md and extracted every determinism claim.
Mapped each CSS/Compiler/Runtime claim to the implementing function and file in the provided evidence.

Identified open questions about compiler-side topological ordering, byte normalization, and the alternative anchor fallback in bundler_html_emit.rs.
